dialogue: Welcome, Owner champion. Ana de Arepas needs your help! Which concepts below are included in the 'Poor Fit' list?
options: Food is not the primary focus
explanation: Bakeries, Desert Shops, Ghost Kitchens, Meal Prep Kitchens, and businesses where food is not the primary focus are all on the Poor Fit list.

dialogue: What are our outbound qualification ICP standards?
options: Min $3k eGMV, existing website, under 10 locations
Min $5k eGMV, no website needed, under 5 locations
Min $1k eGMV, existing website, under 20 locations
explanation: Our ICP standards are: Minimum $3k eGMV, existing website and presence, and under 10 locations.

dialogue: Do we need access to the customer's GBP for them to be successful on Owner?
explanation: Yes! GBP access is strictly required for success on Owner.

dialogue: Where can customers find their GBP login information or gain access?
options: They cannot access GBP unless they have the original login
They can claim it by verifying ownership through Google, even without the original email
explanation: Customers can claim a GBP by saying they own the business and verifying via email. If they don't have access to that email, they can go through Google verification of ownership.

dialogue: Does the customer need to have a Yelp page to use Owner?
explanation: Yes, customers need to have a Yelp page to use Owner.

dialogue: Do we need access to the customer's website domain to move forward?
explanation: We need access to the domain if they have one. It's important but situational.

dialogue: What can the website whois.com/whois be used for?
explanation: WHOIS is used for Domain Host Research — finding out who hosts a customer's domain.

dialogue: What is a fractured domain?
options: A website infected with a virus
Customer has multiple websites that divide visitor traffic
Customer lost market share to competitors
explanation: A fractured domain is when a customer has multiple websites that divide visitor traffic, confusing guests and hurting rankings.

dialogue: Why are fractured domains so bad?
options: Google favors traffic given to a single domain for ranking
Multiple websites make the guest journey unclear
Order volume is typically cut by 50%
explanation: All of the above! Google favors single domains, multiple sites confuse guests, and order volume is typically cut by 50%.

dialogue: Do we need access to the customer's top ranking domain?
options: Yes, but we can use another as an absolute last resort
explanation: Yes, we need the top ranking domain, but we can use another as an absolute last resort.

dialogue: Does the customer HAVE to have a current website to sign with Owner?
explanation: No, they don't need a current website. We can work with customers who don't have one yet.

dialogue: What risk comes with buying a new domain for a customer?
options: An older site may exist creating a fractured domain
New domains take months to rank on Google
explanation: Both! There might be an older site creating a fractured domain, AND new domains can take months for Google to rank.

dialogue: Does the customer continue to own their domain after signing up with Owner?
explanation: Yes! The customer always continues to own their domain.

dialogue: What information is necessary to set up a Stripe account?
options: SSN/ITIN, photo ID, and DOB only
Legal business name and EIN only
All of the above: SSN/ITIN, photo ID, DOB, address, legal business name, EIN, and business contact info
explanation: Setting up Stripe requires everything: SSN or ITIN, photo ID, DOB, address, contact info, legal business name, EIN, and business contact information.

dialogue: Stripe will require a person's information if they cross what % threshold of ownership?
explanation: Stripe requires personal information for anyone who crosses the 20% ownership threshold.

dialogue: What is the Stripe holding period after initial account setup?
explanation: The initial Stripe holding period is 7 Days after account setup.

dialogue: After the initial holding period, how long is the payout processing cycle?
explanation: After the initial hold, Stripe uses a T+2 processing cycle for payouts.

dialogue: How much is the Stripe processing fee?
explanation: The standard Stripe processing fee is 2.9% + 30 cents per transaction.

dialogue: Why are processing fees typically higher for online orders?
explanation: Online orders have higher processing fees due to a higher risk of fraud compared to in-person transactions.

dialogue: For new accounts, who pays the processing fee?
options: Either the guest or customer, at the customer's discretion
explanation: For new accounts, the processing fee is always paid by the customer (the restaurant).

dialogue: What protection do we have against fraudulent CC transactions?
options: Stripe's built-in machine learning fraud detection called 3D Secure
We keep John Wick on retainer
explanation: We use Stripe's built-in machine learning fraud detection product called 3D Secure.

dialogue: How do we handle CC disputes and chargebacks?
options: Customer sorts it with guest and Stripe. If unable, Owner reimburses loss but NOT the $15 Stripe fee
Customer sorts it with guest and Stripe. If unable, Owner reimburses loss AND the $15 Stripe fee
The customer is entirely on their own
explanation: Stripe contacts the customer with the dispute. The customer tries to sort it with the guest and Stripe. If they can't, Owner reimburses the loss AND the $15 Stripe processing fee.

dialogue: What is the Owner fee capped at per order?
explanation: The Owner service fee is capped at $7.50 per order.

dialogue: Who pays the Owner service fee?
options: The fee can be split between customer and guest
explanation: The Owner service fee can be split between the customer (restaurant) and the guest.

dialogue: Does an Owner customer pay sales tax on their subscription fee?
options: Yes, directly to the Sheriff of Nottingham
explanation: Yes, Owner customers pay sales tax on their subscription fee in 9 states.

dialogue: Where can customers get a report of the sales tax they've collected?
options: They must request a W9 from Owner Support
In both Stripe and Owner Dashboard
explanation: Customers can get their sales tax collection report in the Owner Dashboard only.

dialogue: Which POS systems does Owner currently integrate with?
options: Toast, Clover, Square, Lightspeed K, Vela, EPOS
All major POS systems including Menusifu and Aloha
explanation: Owner integrates with Toast, Clover, Square, Lightspeed Series K, Vela, and EPOS.

dialogue: Why would a customer integrate with Square or Clover through Otter when we have native integrations?
options: Some people just want to watch the world burn
explanation: If the customer is already using Otter, they may prefer to keep their existing Otter integration rather than switch.

dialogue: Do our Otter integrations offer both order pushes and menu pushes?
explanation: Yes! Our Otter integrations support both order pushes and menu pushes.

dialogue: Where do integrated customers make changes to their menus?
explanation: It depends on the integration. Different POS integrations have different workflows for menu management.

dialogue: Do we support nested modifiers?
explanation: Yes! We support nested modifiers for all customers regardless of integration type.

dialogue: Do we integrate with any loyalty or gift card solutions?
explanation: No, we do not currently integrate with any external loyalty or gift card solutions.

dialogue: How do our websites improve SEO?
options: Smart keywords, SEO-tagged images, fast load times, and high engagement
Google SEO Premier Program paid partnership
Smart keywords and fast load times only
explanation: Our websites improve SEO through smart keywords, keyword-tagged images, fast load times, and high website engagement through clicks and ordering.

dialogue: What matters the MOST for SEO at Owner?
options: Number of cookies collected and cached
Consistent information about the restaurant across the internet
explanation: Consistent information about the restaurant across the internet is the most vital factor for SEO at Owner.

dialogue: How is what Owner does with websites different from what customers currently do?
options: Design-opinionated for SEO, stay on domain, and design for conversion
10 templates optimized for marketing and auto-capture customer info
explanation: We're design-opinionated sacrificing custom aesthetics for optimal SEO. We stay on domain (no 3P OLO links). We design for conversion through strategic call-to-action and design.

dialogue: Can we guarantee SEO results?
explanation: No, we cannot guarantee SEO results. SEO is influenced by many factors beyond our control.

dialogue: Do customers have to do everything that we recommend for SEO?
explanation: Yes! Customers need to follow our SEO recommendations for the best results.

dialogue: Can customers maintain their other websites (Slice, PopMenu, SquareSpace) and also work with Owner?
options: No — this creates a fractured domain
Yes — more places means more eyes and orders
explanation: No! Maintaining other websites creates a fractured domain, which is bad for SEO and splits traffic.

dialogue: Why can customers not customize the look of the website more?
options: We have an opinionated design philosophy with proven tactics to optimize SEO
We believe every website should look the same to strengthen Owner brand recognition
explanation: We have an opinionated design philosophy with proven tactics to optimize SEO. Custom aesthetics are sacrificed for better results.

dialogue: Are Owner websites guaranteed to be ADA compliant?
explanation: No, Owner websites are not guaranteed to be ADA compliant.

dialogue: Can customers change the font on the website?
options: Yes, we accept any .TTF file upload
Yes, but only our default fonts are available
No, our fonts are set for SEO optimization
explanation: Yes, customers can change fonts, but only our default fonts are available to choose from.

dialogue: How can customers make edits to their website?
options: They can edit it themselves in the Dashboard or reach out to Support
They must mail 12 box tops to David Albers in Palo Alto
explanation: Customers can edit their website themselves in the Dashboard or reach out to Support for help.

dialogue: How can customers accept reservations on Owner websites?
options: Via a form that sends answers by email, embed OpenTable, or link to a reservation site
Via our Owner Reservations product with calendar management
We can embed widgets from any reservation company
explanation: Customers can accept reservations via a form on the website that emails them, by embedding an OpenTable widget, or by linking to a reservation website of their choice.

dialogue: Can Owner integrate Google Forms into our websites?
explanation: No, we cannot integrate Google Forms into Owner websites.

dialogue: Can a customer embed any kind of HTML into our websites?
options: Yes, but only for counters and calendars
Yes, but only for invisible marketing tags and metadata
No, we do not support general custom code
explanation: No, we do not support general custom code or HTML embedding on Owner websites.

dialogue: What versions of a menu can we import into our system?
options: First party website links, 3P Marketplace links, and PDF/Image
Any kind of POS system export
explanation: We can import menus from first party website links (Menufy, BeyondMenu, etc), 3P Marketplace links (DoorDash, EZ Cater, etc), and PDF or Image files.

dialogue: How long does it TYPICALLY take to bring a PDF menu into our system?
explanation: It typically takes 48 hours to bring a PDF menu into our system.

dialogue: How can a customer upload images for their menu?
explanation: Customers can upload menu images through both the Owner Dashboard and by contacting Support.

dialogue: IDEALLY, what percentage of menu items should have images?
explanation: Ideally, 60% or more of menu items should feature high-quality images to drive orders.

dialogue: Can customers have multiple standard OLO menus?
options: Yes, but they can't overlap in availability
No, only one menu is supported
No, but categories can be used as a workaround
explanation: Yes! Customers can have multiple standard OLO menus, but they can't overlap in availability.

dialogue: Can customers have catering menus on their website?
explanation: Yes! Customers can have catering menus on their Owner website.

dialogue: Can customers have in-store menus on their website?
explanation: Kind of — customers can display in-store menus on their website, but only as a PDF upload.

dialogue: Can customers disable automated marketing?
options: Yes, they can disable all or turn certain ones off individually in the Dashboard
Yes, but it's all or nothing in Brand Settings
No, they must be on to drive higher sales
explanation: Yes! Customers can disable all automated campaigns or turn certain ones off individually in the Dashboard.

dialogue: How many SMS marketing blasts can customers send?
options: It depends on how many SMS subscribers they have
Every customer gets 1 SMS blast per month
explanation: The number of SMS blasts depends on how many SMS subscribers the customer has.

dialogue: Can customers create their own marketing emails and texts?
explanation: Yes! Customers can create their own marketing emails and texts.

dialogue: How many marketing emails can customers send?
explanation: Customers can send unlimited marketing emails!

dialogue: Can customers pick and choose which guests they want to send one-off marketing emails to?
options: Yes, via a guest marketing selector list
Yes, based on tags they've put on marketing lists
No, there is currently no segmentation
explanation: No, there is currently no segmentation. Customers cannot selectively target individual guests for one-off emails.

dialogue: Can customers import their existing guest data?
explanation: Yes! Customers can import their existing guest data into the Owner platform.

dialogue: Can a customer offer coupons for pick-up or delivery orders only?
explanation: Yes! Customers can restrict coupons to either pick-up or delivery orders.

dialogue: Can customers control the value of the coupons?
explanation: Yes! Customers have full control over the value of their coupons.

dialogue: Can a customer do a BOGO coupon like Payless Shoes?
explanation: No, BOGO (Buy One Get One) coupons are not supported on the Owner platform.

dialogue: Can guests redeem gift cards in store?
explanation: Yes, guests can redeem gift cards in store — though the process isn't the smoothest.

dialogue: How many points are guests given at enrollment of loyalty?
explanation: Guests receive 100 points just for enrolling in the loyalty program.

dialogue: What is the ratio of loyalty points to dollars spent?
options: 1 point per 10 cents on orders over $5
explanation: The standard ratio is 10 points per $1 spent.

dialogue: Can customers advertise events on an Owner website?
explanation: Yes! Customers can advertise events on their Owner website.

dialogue: What push notifications do we send to guests from our mobile apps?
options: Order delayed, ready for pickup, and coupon offers
Delivery driver received food and delivery complete
explanation: We send push notifications for: order is delayed, order is ready for pickup by guest, and coupon offers.

dialogue: How far do our delivery partners deliver and how much do they charge?
explanation: Uber delivers up to 8 miles at $6.45, and DoorDash delivers up to 5 miles at $7.

dialogue: How much do we recommend a customer charges for delivery?
options: No higher than $3.99, especially on orders over $30
Whatever the customer wants — we have no opinion
explanation: We strongly recommend no higher than $3.99, especially on orders over $30.

dialogue: How are tips on 3rd party delivery orders distributed?
options: Customer selects how tips are divided
Driver gets up to 20% of subtotal, rest goes to restaurant
explanation: The 3rd party driver gets up to 20% of the subtotal as a tip. Anything above that goes to the restaurant.

dialogue: What solution do we offer if there is an issue with a 3P delivery order?
options: Owner will cover the refund amount
The guest needs to call Uber or DoorDash
Owner offers a remake of the food
explanation: Owner will cover the refund amount to ensure guest satisfaction.

dialogue: Does Uber/DoorDash have average delivery times?
explanation: No, Uber and DoorDash do not have guaranteed average delivery times.

dialogue: Can customers use their own drivers?
explanation: Yes! Customers can use their own drivers for delivery.

dialogue: Can customers set an order minimum for delivery?
explanation: Yes! Customers can set a minimum order amount for delivery orders.

dialogue: How does delivering alcohol work on Owner?
options: 3P is notified to capture Photo ID upon delivery
We currently do not offer alcohol delivery until further notice
explanation: We currently do not offer alcohol delivery until further notice.

dialogue: Is alcohol delivery available in all states?
explanation: No, alcohol delivery is not available in all states due to varying state regulations.

dialogue: Can customers connect the Owner tablet to multiple printers? Can multiple tablets connect to one printer?
explanation: No! It's one tablet to one printer. No multiple connections in either direction.

dialogue: What brand of printer does Owner provide for our customers?
explanation: We provide the Star Micronics TSP printer for our customers.

dialogue: What brand of tablet does Owner provide for our customers?
explanation: We provide the Lenovo Yoga Tab 11 for our customers.

dialogue: What is our app used on the tablet called?
explanation: Our tablet app is called the Kitchen App.

dialogue: Can a customer add the tablet app to a different device or upload other apps onto an Owner tablet?
options: Yes, from the official Play store with family friendly ratings
Yes, but only approved productivity apps
Yes, with CSM permission and override code
explanation: No! Customers cannot add the Kitchen App to other devices or install other apps on the Owner tablet.

dialogue: Does the customer have to manually 'accept' catering orders?
explanation: No! Catering orders do not require manual acceptance.

dialogue: When does Owner stop accepting orders on our OLO?
options: Custom 'Stop Order Time' field in Dashboard
explanation: Owner stops accepting orders at closing time minus prep/delivery time to ensure orders can be fulfilled.

dialogue: Can a customer sell marijuana on their OLO?
explanation: No! Customers cannot sell marijuana through Owner's online ordering system.

dialogue: How does a guest place a catering order online?
options: On the regular menu, designating it as catering at checkout
On a separate catering menu OLO page
explanation: Guests place catering orders on a separate catering menu OLO page, not the regular menu.

dialogue: Can customers sell merchandise or anything not for immediate consumption?
explanation: Yes! Customers can sell merchandise and non-food items on their Owner website.

dialogue: How quickly do customers get conversion cards?
options: Within one week of going live
explanation: Conversion cards take approximately 10 business days to arrive.

dialogue: Who is Invisible?
options: Our BPO (outsourced team) that helps with menu and funnel builds
explanation: Invisible is our BPO (Business Process Outsourcing) team that helps with menu and funnel builds.

dialogue: What ways can a customer contact Support?
options: Phone, SMS, AI Chat, and Email
Through their old XDR via Slack
explanation: Customers can contact Support via phone call, SMS text, AI-powered chat, and email.

dialogue: Final question, champion! What is the name of Eva's mini-dachshund?
explanation: Eva's mini-dachshund is named Nathan! You've completed the FKAT Trials!
